prompt_engine.description:
🚀 PROMPT ENGINE: Execute prompts with methodology guidance and ground-truth validation.

SYNTAX:
- >>prompt_id key:"value"              # Run prompt with arguments
- >>prompt * N                         # Repeat N times (chain shorthand)
- >>step1 --> >>step2                  # Chain multiple steps
- @Framework >>prompt                  # Apply methodology (CAGEERF, ReACT, etc.)
- :: verify:"npm test" :full          # Shell validation (5 attempts, 5min)
- :: verify:"cmd" loop:true            # Autonomous until pass

REPETITION:
- * N unfolds to chain with SAME args (>>p arg * 3 = >>p arg --> >>p arg --> >>p arg)
- For DIFFERENT args per step, use explicit chain: >>p arg1 --> >>p arg2
- Each step receives previous output via context propagation

PRESETS: :fast (1 try), :full (5 tries), :extended (10 tries)
MODIFIERS: %clean (no injection), %lean (gates only), %judge (preview)
prompt_engine.shortDescription: Execute prompts with validation and methodology

parameters.command: Prompt ID to expand. Format: >>prompt_id key="value" | Chains: >>s1 --> >>s2 | Modifiers first: @Framework :: "criteria" %clean/%lean | Shell verify: :: verify:"cmd" :preset
parameters.chain_id: Resume token (chain-{prompt} or chain-{prompt}#runNumber). RESUME: chain_id + user_response only. Omit command.
parameters.user_response: Your completed output from executing the previous step. Paste your work here when resuming a chain. Use with chain_id; do not include command when resuming.
parameters.gate_verdict: Gate review verdict with flexible format support. Primary: 'GATE_REVIEW: PASS|FAIL - reason'. Also accepts: 'GATE PASS - reason', 'GATE_REVIEW: FAIL: reason', 'PASS - reason' (minimal).
parameters.gate_action: User choice after gate retry limit exhaustion. 'retry' resets attempt count for another try, 'skip' bypasses the failed gate and continues, 'abort' stops chain execution entirely.

gates.description:
Quality gates for output validation. Four formats supported:

**1. Registered IDs** (strings): Use predefined gates like 'code-quality', 'research-quality'.

**2. Quick Gates** (RECOMMENDED for LLM-generated validation): `{name, description}` - Create named, domain-specific checks on the fly. Example: `{name: 'Source Quality', description: 'All sources must be official docs'}`.

**3. Full Definitions**: Complete schema with severity, criteria[], pass_criteria[], guidance for production workflows.

**4. Shell Verification** (ground-truth validation): `:: verify:"command"` runs shell commands; exit 0 = PASS.
   - **Presets**: `:fast` (1 attempt, 30s), `:full` (5 attempts, 5min), `:extended` (10 attempts, 10min)
   - **Options**: `max:N` (attempts), `timeout:N` (seconds), `loop:true` (autonomous Stop hook)
   - **Example**: `>>fix-bug :: verify:"npm test" :full loop:true`

parameters.force_restart: Restart chain from step 1, ignoring cached state.

frameworkAware.enabled: 🚀 PROMPT ENGINE [FRAMEWORK]: Returns prompts with methodology guidance injected. You apply the methodology yourself.
frameworkAware.disabled: 🚀 PROMPT ENGINE: Returns prompts without methodology injection. Raw template expansion only.

triggerExamples: User: '>>analyze this code' → prompt_engine(command: '>>analyze this code')

resource_manager.description:
🗂️ RESOURCE MANAGER: CRUD for prompts, gates, methodologies, checkpoints.

resource_manager(resource_type, action, id?, name?, description?)

TYPES: prompt | gate | methodology | checkpoint
ACTIONS: create | update | delete | list | inspect | reload | guide | switch | history | rollback | compare | clear

UPDATE: update(id, description:"new") — only provided fields change. inspect(id, detail:"full") to see current content first.

VERSIONING: history (view versions), rollback (restore), compare (diff versions)
CHECKPOINT: create (save state), rollback (restore), list (show all), delete (remove), clear (remove all)

CREATION GUIDES:
• >>create_methodology - Framework creation with validation
• >>create_gate - Quality gate creation
• >>create_prompt - Prompt/chain creation
resource_manager.shortDescription: Manage prompts, gates, methodologies, and checkpoints

parameters.resource_type: Type of resource to manage. Routes to appropriate handler.
parameters.action: Operation to perform. Type-specific: analyze_type/guide (prompt), switch (methodology), clear (checkpoint). Versioning: history/rollback/compare (prompt/gate/methodology). Checkpoint: create/rollback/list/delete/clear.
parameters.id: Resource identifier. Required for create, update, delete, inspect, reload, switch.
parameters.name: Human-friendly name for the resource (create/update).
parameters.enabled_only: Filter list to enabled resources only. Default: true.

frameworkAware.enabled: 🗂️ RESOURCE MANAGER [FRAMEWORK]: Operations reflect active methodology context.
frameworkAware.disabled: 🗂️ RESOURCE MANAGER: Standard resource lifecycle operations.

skills_sync.description:
🧩 SKILLS SYNC: Keep client skills aligned with canonical resources.

ACTIONS: status | export | sync | diff | pull | clone

Use for status checks, reconciliation, drift reporting, and round-trip edits.

parameters.action: Operation: status (inspect config/manifests), export (write skills), sync (reconcile to registrations with optional prune), diff (show drift and optional .patch output), pull (merge exported prose edits back to canonical YAML), clone (create canonical resources from SKILL.md).
parameters.client: Target client id. Use one of: claude-code, cursor, codex, opencode, or all.
parameters.scope: Output scope for client directories. Default: user.
parameters.prune: For sync: when true (default), remove stale managed skills not present in current registrations.
parameters.dry_run: For export/sync/pull/clone: show planned changes without writing files.
parameters.output: For diff: write .patch files to this directory instead of stdout only.
parameters.file: For clone: path to the source SKILL.md file. Required for clone action.
parameters.category: For clone: target category for prompt resources. Default: general.
parameters.preview: For pull: show unified diffs without writing changes.
parameters.force: For clone: overwrite existing resource directory.

frameworkAware.enabled: 🧩 SKILLS SYNC [FRAMEWORK]: Skill sync actions with active methodology context available in surrounding workflow.
frameworkAware.disabled: 🧩 SKILLS SYNC: Skill sync actions without methodology context.

system_control.description:
⚙️ SYSTEM CONTROL: Retrieves system status, switches frameworks, manages gates, and provides analytics.

ACTIONS: status | framework | gates | analytics | config | maintenance | guide | session

Returns: Current state, configurations, or operation confirmations.

parameters.action: The operation to perform: status (runtime overview), framework (switch/enable/disable methodologies), gates (manage quality gates), analytics (usage metrics), config (view/modify settings), maintenance (restart), guide (get recommendations), session (manage execution sessions).
parameters.operation: Sub-command for the selected action (e.g., framework switch/list/enable/disable; gates enable/disable/status/health/list; session list/clear/inspect).
parameters.session_id: Target session ID or chain ID for session operations.
parameters.framework: Target framework for switch operations. Use system_control(action:'framework', operation:'list') to see available frameworks.
parameters.reason: Audit reason for framework/gate toggles or admin actions.
parameters.persist: When true, gate/framework enable/disable changes are also written to config.json.
parameters.search_query: Filter gates by keyword (matches ID, name, or description). Use with gates:list action.

frameworkAware.enabled: ⚙️ SYSTEM CONTROL [FRAMEWORK]: Status includes active methodology context. Guide reflects current framework.
frameworkAware.disabled: ⚙️ SYSTEM CONTROL: Standard system status without methodology context.
